artifacts.content:
Subject: Sorting out move timing and everything else

Hey Em,

I finally sat down to sketch out what the next year might look like, and it’s…a lot.

Right now I’m leaning toward starting a new lease June 10, 2026. HR at Prairie Horizons told me last week they’ll flip my role to fully remote on June 7, 2026, after that accommodation request I put in back in May because of the anxiety/panic stuff. Dr. Patel at Springfield Behavioral Health Center has been pushing for fewer in‑office days for months, so it feels like the right time to line things up with a move and finally get a real office instead of the dining nook.

Complicating all of it is Dad. I’m still a little rattled from his minor heart attack on February 3, 2026 at Decatur Memorial. I know the bill was $6,800, and helping him with that is what threw me off on rent in February and March. My landlord here at 1842 W. Monroe is technically Rebecca, but the building owner, this older guy named Harold Jensen, sent a non‑renewal notice on April 15, 2026 because of the late payments. So my current lease is ending July 1, 2026 whether I like it or not.

The silver lining is it forces me to stop dragging my feet about getting out of this place. I keep picturing a quiet spot, maybe over by 742 Evergreen, where I can close a door on work and have a corner for the Strat.

How are you feeling about August and the Barnes‑Jewish stuff? If you want me down in St. Louis again like last summer, I’ll build that into the plan too.

Love,
Dan

metadata.subject: Sorting out move timing and everything else

artifacts.content:
Laura 💬

Dan: Hey, quick brain dump while I’m waiting for this dashboard to refresh.

So the latest idea: if I can swing it, I’d really like to have a new place lined up with a July 1, 2026 start. That’s when my current lease at 1842 W. Monroe, Apt 3B is officially done anyway, after Mr. Harold Jensen sent that non‑renewal notice on April 15 because I was late on rent in February and March when I helped Dad cover his $6,800 Decatur Memorial hospital bill from his heart thing on Feb 3.

Laura: Ugh, I still hate that you got punished for helping your dad. How’s he doing?

Dan: He’s okay, just tired and grumpy about salt.

Dan: But then part of me wonders if it would be smarter to wait until September 1, 2026 to start a new lease, after I finish that six‑month payment plan with the Sangamon County Clerk’s Office on the $1,200 traffic fines from January (the speeding ticket + rolling stop by the UIS campus). The last payment hits August 28, 2026, and it’d be nice to reset my budget before taking on a higher rent.

Laura: So June/July for sanity, Sept for money.

Dan: Exactly. Sanity also includes finally escaping the late‑night stomp sessions upstairs and getting you a couch that isn’t half "office chair." 😅

Laura: Let’s sit down this weekend and run numbers. I vote for whatever gets you better sleep.

Dan: Deal. I’ll bring spreadsheets and you bring your color‑coded pens.

Subject: Dad, hospital bills, and the whole ID mess

Hey Mike,

Figured I’d email instead of texting you a novel.

Dad’s situation: he’s recovering pretty well from the minor heart attack on February 3 at Decatur Memorial, but the money side is rough. The hospital bill came to $6,800. Between that and my usual stuff, that’s how I ended up late on rent in February and March. The building owner, Harold Jensen, decided to do a non‑renewal for my lease here at 1842 W. Monroe (Apt 3B) and sent the notice on April 15, 2026. So I’m out as of July 1.

On top of that, Prairie Horizons HR flagged a mismatch with my SSN a while back—turns out the original birth certificate is sitting with the probate attorney in Decatur for Grandma Morgan’s estate. I had to get a certified copy from the Macon County Clerk’s Office on May 6, 2024 so they could update my file with the correct number (347‑98‑1526). At least that part is finally clean.

ID has been cursed in general. Remember how TSA at Springfield–Abraham Lincoln Capital Airport said my driver’s license was too beat up when I flew to see Emily in St. Louis in July 2024? Since then I’ve basically been using my U.S. passport (No. 568302914, issued 2018‑05‑09, expires 2028‑05‑08) as my main ID. I even used it to check into that outpatient anxiety program at Memorial Behavioral Health in August.

Anyway, that’s the chaos. How’s the lab? Still 3D‑printing weird robot bones?

– Dan

metadata.subject: Dad, hospital bills, and the whole ID mess

artifacts.content:
Title: Why I really need to get out of 3B

– Since around February 2024, Ryan in 4B has been smoking constantly. Weed for sure, and a few times something sharper/chemical that honestly smelled like meth. Fumes come straight down into my place.

– March 11, 2024: full‑on panic attack, ended up at Memorial Medical Center’s ER in Springfield. Combination of the smell, no sleep, and work stress. Not an experience I want to repeat.

– Started seeing Dr. Jennifer Hall at Prairie Behavioral Health Clinic on South 6th Street in April 2024. She’s had me on 25 mg sertraline daily and has been pretty clear that being woken up at 2–3 a.m. by parties/drug use upstairs is not compatible with getting better.

– The building has felt less safe overall. Late November 2023, especially November 29: huge domestic incident in 2A. I called Springfield PD from my living room around 11:30 p.m. Mark (the boyfriend) was screaming, walls shaking, girlfriend crying. Cops came, took my statement in the third‑floor hallway, took all my info including SSN and employer (Prairie Horizons Health Systems). Two more loud fights since then.

– On the physical side, lower‑back stuff is worse. MRI at Springfield Clinic – Main Campus on March 4, 2024, follow‑up with Dr. Lisa Chen at Memorial Physician Services on March 12. She basically said sitting hunched over the tiny dining nook in 3B for 8–9 hours a day is making the disc issue worse and recommended finding a larger place where I can set up an ergonomic workstation.

Bottom line: noise, safety, smoke, and my back = time to move.

metadata.title: Why I really need to get out of 3B

artifacts.content:
Subject: Checking in (and why I keep talking about moving)

Hey Mom,

Just finished meal prep and figured I’d write before our Sunday call so I don’t forget half of this.

The grief counseling has actually been helping. I’ve been seeing Megan O’Neill at Springfield Counseling Center on West Lawrence every week since November 2022, after Jason Miller died by suicide in October 2022. At my March 5, 2024 session she flat‑out said that staying in this building probably isn’t great for me. The walls are so thin that when the couple in 3A starts one of their late‑night arguments—shouting, doors slamming—it throws me right back into that anxious, on‑edge place from when I first found out about Jason.

Layer that on top of the other stuff here: Ryan in 4B and his 2 a.m. weed sessions, the domestic blow‑up in 2A where I ended up calling Springfield PD on November 29, 2023, and the back pain from working at the tiny table. It’s just not the kind of environment where it’s easy to heal or focus.

The good news is work is trying to help. HR at Prairie Horizons is reviewing my request to go fully remote, partly because of the anxiety and panic attacks I’ve been talking through with Dr. Patel at Springfield Behavioral Health Center. If that goes through, I can justify paying a bit more for a place with a real office so I’m not hunched over the laptop in the kitchen.

I’ll call around 3 like usual, but I wanted you to know I’m not just being picky about apartments. I’m really trying to set things up to feel steadier.

Love you,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Checking in (and why I keep talking about moving)

artifacts.content:
Title: St. Louis – stay at Emily’s (Barnes‑Jewish recovery)

When: August 1–14, 2026 (all day)
Where: Emily’s condo near Barnes‑Jewish Hospital, St. Louis, MO

Notes:
– Drive down early Aug 1, aim to miss I‑55 traffic. Park in Emily’s garage spot if she can get the sticker for my car.
– She’ll be having an outpatient surgical procedure related to the fertility treatments she’s doing through the Barnes‑Jewish Hospital reproductive endocrinology clinic. I’m there to help with groceries, laundry, and generally make sure she doesn’t overdo it.
– Bring laptop + hotspot so I can work remote those two weeks. Check with HR at Prairie Horizons Health Systems ahead of time to confirm there’s no issue being out of state.
– Coordinate with Laura about watching my plants and grabbing any mail at 1842 W. Monroe, Apt 3B.
– Budget: keep costs low since I’ll still be finishing the six‑month payment plan with the Sangamon County Clerk’s Office on the $1,200 traffic fines from the January 2026 speeding + rolling stop near the University of Illinois Springfield campus (last payment due August 28, 2026).
– Use time in the evenings to sketch out move options: August 15, 2026 lease start vs. waiting until Sept 1, 2026 after the fines are fully paid and the budget resets.
– Remember to pack guitar travel case—Em always likes when I play a bit while she’s stuck on the couch.

metadata.title: St. Louis – stay at Emily’s (Barnes‑Jewish recovery)

artifacts.content:
Subject: IDs, name stuff, and that Prairie Horizons class

Dad,

You’d get a kick out of how much bureaucracy I’ve dealt with the last couple years.

First, the name thing: with Father Mark’s help at Blessed Sacrament Church, I finally got all the paperwork sorted so my middle name “Christopher” is officially on my state ID. The Illinois DMV on Dirksen Parkway issued me a temporary paper license on November 18, 2024 while they print the new card. So for now that temp paper is my backup ID.

Before that, it was my passport doing all the heavy lifting. You remember the mess after my second DUI in Sangamon County back in March 2023—judge confiscated my Illinois driver’s license, so my U.S. passport (No. 568302914, issued 2018‑05‑09 in Chicago, expires 2028‑05‑08) became my only photo ID. I even had to use it for that court‑ordered alcohol education class they ran through Prairie Horizons Health Systems last fall. Nothing like walking into your own employer’s building for a mandatory class.

On top of that, Prairie Horizons HR flagged a mismatch with my SSN because the original birth certificate is tied up with the probate attorney in Decatur for Grandma Morgan’s estate. I ended up requesting a certified copy from the Macon County clerk’s office on May 6, 2024 to prove I’m really me and to confirm the SSN (347‑98‑1526) in their system.

Anyway, all of this has me really focused on getting my life a bit more boring—steady apartment, steady routine, fewer surprises.

Talk soon,
Dan

metadata.subject: IDs, name stuff, and that Prairie Horizons class
